author: [AEP] Moustacheride
content: Double checked with the SAGE game manuals. Didn't find anything regarding taxes or smth that will help the team to extract part of the ATLAS from circulation in order to use it for further distribution of rewards (in ATLAS) for completing quests and other activities. Through taxes or rental fees we can slow down the speed of ATLAS emission, since part of the new reward will be paid from tokens that we previously retired.

author: 𝕘𝕣𝕚𝕤𝕥
content:
Fam, it is magical thinking but it is perfect timing for Star Atlas that a 5 hour Eve Online video from Down the Rabbit Hole dropped this week.

I’ll post the link in off topic, but for anyone who hasn’t had the time to explore the history of Eve this might be an easy way to digest it all.  Make sure to read both Empires of Eve eventually.

My first Tigu ban was in 21 posting Eve vid links (rip prime mover). I believe Star Atlas will take the baton and create a virtual world orders of magnitude beyond Eve.

It’s been an amazing week and seeing this vid was the cherry on top.

Fitness covers a lot more than hitting the gym  And there is a good reason they focus on movement, as that is by far the simplest to build, and also the most difficult to successfully “fake”.

It’s the same reason why we have StepN and competitors who reward physical movement in the form of walking & running. There are smart algorithms that can detect fake data coming in. That is a ton more difficult with lifting weights. So it is more or less simply a technical limitation

author: defiantwyvern
content: I think you not read all my messages in Foundation-room. I am not speaking about what we will see in the game sometime in the future. I am writing about some not too hard updates inside current SAGE game, which can speed up the game economy.   At the moment POLIS doesn't have any utility, except staking... And ATLAS can be used to purchase something on the marketplace, staking and pay fees for transactions. If we want bigger turnover and demand, there should be more activities inside the game, where we can use our tokens now...  Anyway, thanks for your comments

author: ! SH | Bohemian ⪛⦿⫺
content: In a few sentences. --- It will be cool if in SAGE we can have a more dynamic economic model. One of the way to speed it up is to limit the player’s ability to extract all resources without restrictions and on all planets, and force him to choose the planets and resources that he would like to mine. Each land plot will have rental fee depending on the tire and rarity of resources (should be locked in POLIS), as well as regular monthly taxes (will be paid in ATLAS). This way we will get more reasons to trade, increase the turnover of POLIS/ATLAS, as well as a number of other advantages and some disadvantages (read full version)

author: odvb
content:
Got a question. For live races or intense battle royales, particularly in "death races", enhancing exposure and elevating production value in feeds or streams could be achieved by deploying multiple virtual cameras across race tracks or arenas. Implementing observer modes that allow seamless switching between players would also be super nice.
It would be fantastic to develop an interface for this purpose, possibly even making it modding-compatible. This way, community developers could contribute, utilizing it not only for gaming but also for video production. Such a feature would enrich the viewing experience, benefiting content creators and live streamers alike.
The key question is whether this is feasible or if one would need to manage several clients simultaneously, which could pose challenges for the average content creator. Is it easy enough to implement something like that in UE5 or is it maybe already in the future versions?

author: odvb
content: Been spitballing what a wager racing situation would look like.   Rather then burn I think a good high stakes wager would be a Winner Take All race.  If I lost and my ship was burned that would bother me, but if I lost and knew the better player won my ship it would suck. But not the same as burning the ship.   In this mode we would risk the ships we race with and the chance at winning all the ships would be a rush.  Perhaps the highest level would be no limit wagers. The person who is so sure they will win can put up a few more ships (they would be also broadcasting their confidence with this move).  Such races could be open to any SA asset. Not just ships. Put skins, R4…whatever…  The wager could have an open sign up, with a specific race time. In order to race you put up your ship and anything else and once at least one more person signs up the race happens. Maybe only two people are on the track but even that would be fun to play or watch or wager on.  If the community doesn’t want to play this way we could also have layers of buy in races. 1k atlas, 5k atlas, 10k atlas—Winner Take All.  We could have the high stakes races be on multiple tracks, 3 race format with tie-breakers if need be.   I’m down for Winner Take All. That may change once I lose and start giving my ships away. Lol.

In this mode we would risk the ships we race with and the chance at winning all the ships would be a rush. Perhaps the highest level would be no limit wagers. The person who is so sure they will win can put up a few more ships (they would be also broadcasting their confidence with this move). Such races could be open to any SA asset. Not just ships. Put skins, R4…whatever… The wager could have an open sign up, with a specific race time. In order to race you put up your ship and anything else and once at least one more person signs up the race happens. Maybe only two people are on the track but even that would be fun to play or watch or wager on. If the community doesn’t want to play this way we could also have layers of buy in races. 1k atlas, 5k atlas, 10k atlas—Winner Take All. We could have the high stakes races be on multiple tracks, 3 race format with tie-breakers if need be. I’m down for Winner Take All. That may change once I lose and start giving my ships away. Lol.

author: ! SH | Bohemian ⪛⦿⫺
content: It's all about priorities.  Game governance is irrelevant if we don't have enough gamers.  I've built 15 companies (most of them ecommerce) and when we launch, we spend 30% of our income in marketing/advertising to fuel growth.  Even now 10 years later, we're spending around 25%.  We know that the runway is tight and they don't really have any marketing budget.  I think the key question the DAO needs to be asking themselves at all times is: "Where's the most impactful place we can spend these funds?"  Once we have a great product that attracts and keeps users, the answer is 100% marketing.  Game governance will become more important with time.  Right now, we have Danny, Chypto, Michael and Chris that are doing a phenomenal job.

author: [AEP] Mendizo
content: Redbox is still around, interesting thing is that they're growing the part of the business where they are actually servicing kiosks for others. They realize their foray into streaming is going to be a hard road, so they're actually getting into being a field service provider. Glad to see they could make that strategic pivot and not just die on the vine hanging onto movies... well have to see how they do long term.

author: [AEP] Mendizo
content: But it does have some adjacency here, which is sometimes it's good to have a B2B line of business, not just B2C. A long time ago now I'd asked Michael and the team if they were considering becoming a platform company, and sell the tools they've built to other projects. I definitely get the challenge with being spread thin, but that could still be a viable path to help with cashflow at some point.

author: [Λ] Sawyn [BR] [STAR]
content: From what was hinted the claim stakes can be placed on a "land plot". It is just a stake that allows you claim and to start building your land based operation center. The first building is the command center. The space station is in the orbit. If you do not have a space station the freighter ship needs to land on the "land" to move cargo. If you have space station then the ship takes it directly from the space with out entering the atmosphere. This save time and fuel. This is how I understand it. There may be more nuances that I am not aware of.

author: [AEP] Mendizo
content: thanks for sharing. a lot of us on the game production team have histories with highly customizable avatar systems and they can honestly lead to a really ugly game. so for the first waves we want to have a bit more control over the output so we don't get too much wackiness and just blends into any other game with a highly customizable indistinct wearables system. Some options no matter how much you curate them will just look ugly, make the game look bad, make the production team look bad and then turn people off from the game which drops the ccus and hurts everyone heh. so we do want to maintain the base level standard. the randomization is still there when minting but we will be excluding some of the most heinous combinations and colors.

author: PG I BULK
content: @SW4GN3R both actually. so the main flow is you enter the main server where everyone flows through. then you can either book a public event through a scheduler. or start a side lobby that is private/public up to a cap of players. the public events will have greater perks, but the side lobbies don't require waiting if there's no opening. also in the public events you will have to travel to those locations whereas the side lobbies you can access and tracks instantly.

author: 𝕘𝕣𝕚𝕤𝕥
content:
I agree. Death race has the chance to be the first time people “get” this project. Add in wagers and another niche is here (it’s not betting, it’s like fantasy sports).

Third person shooter drops as PVE and that’s awesome. People can practice and get a handle on gunplay and movement tech. Our little advanced Aim Labs or Kovaaks. Then PVP drops and it gets better.

And some of these players will drill down, learn about all the other modules and know this is a journey…..some will want to accumulate assets and get into labs. Then Starbased and eventually combat drops and there are multiple niches for people to focus on. 

Combat racing and 3rd person shooter also could be descent streamer content.

author: ZiggyMUD
content: @GoldenLover | BubblePop really imo the referral system isn't really working yet as I've only had 2-3 referrals so far. My guess is a majority of people are referring themselves still or people close to them. Also by putting a referral code in a tweet, I find that existing community members don't always share my videos or content as much as when there is not one. So for now if I'm trying to spread the word about Star Atlas I find it better to not link my referral code. So I create content not for financial gain but to help ensure people keep hearing about Star Atlas hoping the community grows. It would be massively more profitable to be playing SAGE Labs, but I keep creating for the greater good and hopefully future potential.

author: ! SH | Bohemian ⪛⦿⫺
content:
Many times this is taken for granted.There's a point in which the Content Creator (even for regular social media user & Discord server members) that the sweat/effort cost surpasses any goodwill or fun.
Validation through Likes/Views/RTs and shootouts only go so far, and eventually, the financial costs forces them to withdraw from the scene.Elon Musk wants to fix this with X, but I'm afraid it'll just be like a version of YT.  Any contribution should be rewarded in some form; ppl desperately need a New Social Media that surpasses what the Fediverse has began. 

Great work like always, Siggy.

author: ! SH | Bohemian ⪛⦿⫺
content: the way it worked was that, when you went through a portal to his site, he also launched the star atlas webpage with his referral link. doesn't have to be the marketplace, just the main SA landing page I think is good enough. That webpage loads, and the cookie w/ the referral is stored (locally). then if you make a purchase on the marketplace after that (even if you close the popped up SA page and come back later), it would lock in that referral (on chain)

author: Buzzec
content:
Quick history lesson for anyone wondering about this,
In the beginning of Solana rent was collected every epoch at a rate per byte in an account (ex 10 lamports/byte epoch). Rent exemption was added to allow for accounts to have an infinite lifespan, it was defined as having 2 years worth of rent in an account. As time went on it was found that the vast majority of accounts were rent exempt and the rent collection process was quite slow in validators so non-rent exempt account creation was blocked. The only accounts that were not rent-exempt were grandfathered in. Now that it's been >2 years there are no non-rent exempt accounts left (any non-rent exempt account has less than 2 years of rent by defenition) so that code can be removed.

reply.preview: Original message was deleted

author: DominicVainMod
content: The same 10 wallets are placing a ton of small orders in Copper Ore and Carbon Ore.  They could have various reasons for doing so.   I'm not abusing the marketplace so I can't know for certain but these are my thoughts.  1) They can push the visibility of larger orders away, so they can then place a big order at the top that is always seen.  e.g. if i want to sell 500k but there is a 10M order up, ill put in 10 small orders, push the 10M order away, then put in my 500k order at the top.  2) They are using it to push ask price down so they can come in and buy the goods at a cheaper price from the seller.  3) seller is forced to sell at a lower price to have visibility  4) market becomes annoying so players sell into buy orders  5) horrible for buyers to interact with sell orders.  people stop playing?   @GoldenLover | BubblePop any thoughts here?

author: DominicVainMod
content: well lets say seller A has 10M and Seller B has 2M.   If Buyer A wants to buy 3M, they may go straight to seller A.    So Seller B puts a shitload of fake orders in to hide seller A 10M, then places 2M on top.  Buyer A doesn't see the 10M order so he just buys 2M and is fine with it.  (Just one example)

author: [AEP] Boen
content:
I think use of Polis / PVP for starbases, to create political undermining of opposition forces, to dictate social alignment/tax/economic issues in areas of your faction or your guild/alliance/syndicate controls are all fascinating and yes many levels of depth can be created over the next year and after (let's even consider PVP voting power as a way to create improved 'spy/ambassador' influence within opposing regions where you don't have faction / land representation but rather ambassadorial/spy representation)

I think though the one area of more clarity/discussion I would like (especially as most of us typically lock polis for 5 years though and so are making decisions now) is around if how my Polis locked under say my Mud or Oni character may have influence on Ustur region related issues - ie what is that faction 'crossover' level of influence and when does it come into play? If that cross over is high, then frankly I may lock more Polis under say an Oni character, and then still benefit from high Oni region influence while also having influence in the Ustur / Mud region. Or, should I concentrate all my PVP in one faction type. 
I think most of us will concentrate fleet action/crafting/mining within one faction, but we may emphasize politicial influence in all three factions using PVP (depending on the level of crossover influence)

author: odvb
content: Not trying to revive the BOT conversation. We all know SA's position (as you mentioned).   But I believe strongly that there should be a SA Process to get MODs approved - especially the ones that add custom scripts to automate original functionalities.  In most AAA games, using a MOD disqualifies gamers from earning official rewards.  With Web3+ games, this subject needs to be revisited even if it doesn't affect the SA economy much because it opens the doors to economic manipulation and strategic positioning (for a few) as the game transitions.

reply.preview: Not trying to revive the BOT conversation. We all know SA's position (as you mentioned). But I believe strongly that there should be a SA Process to get MODs approved - especially the ones that add custom scripts to automate original functionalities. In most AAA games, using a MOD disqualifies gamers from earning official rewards. With Web3+ games, this subject needs to be revisited even if it doesn't affect the SA economy much because it opens the doors to economic manipulation and strategic positioning (for a few) as the game transitions.

author: [AEP] Mandalorian (Djin Djarin)
content: I support automation. Especially to the extent it is distributed to all players. Automation isn't the problem. Assymetric distribution of automation is. Does anyone care about SCV automation in Starcraft? No. Granted, the process on verification and validation of these external tools does need to improve. We don't want anyone to get compromised. But to some extent, Star Atlas Account should be an initial improvement. And validation is a key process we will be working on improving going forward.  The whole point of an open, distributed, and composable game system is co-creeation. And we're seeing that at play already. This has always been the vision.

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content: We share this vision.   Your support for automation in gaming, especially when it's equitably distributed, resonates with a forward-thinking approach that I've come to love in SA. The asymmetry in the distribution of automation tools is indeed a significant issue, as it can create unfair advantages and disrupt the balance in competitive games. In the context of Starcraft, the automation of SCVs is a well-accepted norm, as it doesn't detract from the game's strategic elements. However, the challenge lies in ensuring that all automation tools undergo official (rigorous) verification and validation. This is crucial not only for maintaining fair play but also for protecting users from security (or ethically questionable) risks.  I look forward to the introduction of Star Atlas Account. I'm glad that it'll offer an improved framework for automation that integrates better safety and fairness checks.  The vision of an open, distributed, and composable game system aligns with the growing trend of co-creation in the gaming world. By allowing players and developers to collaboratively shape the gaming experience, SA is moving towards a more inclusive and dynamic gaming ecosystem. IMO, this model not only enhances creativity and engagement but also ensures that advancements in AI & automation are utilized in a way that benefits the entire SA gaming community.   Thank you, Michael.

Thank you, Michael.

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content:
The nature of these on-chain game system implies that we won't be able to prevent third party development leveraging them. With respect to automation, we'd follow the same philosophy that we do within Labs. If they're open and accessible to everyone, we won't need to take any account-based actions against the users employing them. 

In terms of account management via cli, the assets will still be visually represented in the game. I'm not sure if that's where your concern lies. But just because the user is interactivng via cli, doesn't mean those assets will be hidden from other players. And the risks assumed by those automated accounts will intrinsically be higher than those for actively managed accounts. Especially in those regions where economic incentives are highest, i.e. MRZ/HRZ.

reply.preview: Original message was deleted

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content: Separately, we're designing the game to disincentivize multi-accounting at every layer, to the fullest extent possible. And will continue refining the game to maximize single-account usage. So "botters" should have no benefit over an individual player. And at the end of the day, account potential is directly correlated with asset ownership. There is no way around it. So they are either purchasing sufficient assets to utilize in game (to the benefit of every other stakeholder in the ecosystem), or they are underutilizing the potential of their automation tooling.

author: PG I BULK
content: Absolutely. Steam would be great, and @bunthius does have plans for console support, but that's probably much later in the development path. I don't think it would make sense to go to console in a pre-alpha or even beta state. The average user isn't going to immerse themselves in the background and context of our development philosophy. They expect the game to be finalized when they download. The fastest path to F2P, and most economically sound, is through lending/rental/scholarship markets. And even that will take some serious educating for the mainstream user.   I've kind of loosely mentioned this, but November will be the first month we've been cash flow positive since Feb 2022. It's a massive accomplishment, and very promising. And that's with 2000 DAU. At 20,000 DAU, we are likely cash flow positive in perpetuity, which is a fairly low milestone to achieve, especially with everything up and coming. So we don't need to hit mass market to be a success and have the ability to deliver the full vision.

author: [AEP] Boen
content: Sure, but if people only look at it from an Atlas perspective then absolutely it will appear prices are falling. Atlas is great as a transaction currency, but it adds another variable to determining economic longevity of your operations. This is why companies that have a significant portion of their business overseas require foreign exchange hedging, so that they aren't getting squashed by fluctuating currency levels relative to their home currency. Any long term operation needs to understand its cost basis in a 'fixed price' unit manner (barring inflation of course)
